items.summary: Add at least 2 new distinct enemy character types beyond Sukuna to the 3D fighting game. Each new enemy should have a unique name, distinct 3D character mesh (multi-part articulated body using grouped meshes in Character3D.tsx or a new character component), unique color scheme and emissive material highlights, and its own set of stats (health, speed, attack damage, attack range) defined in the level configuration in frontend/src/types/game.ts. New enemy types should appear in later levels or alongside Sukuna enemies as the levels progress.

Add at least 1 new playable character option for the player, beyond UG. The player should be able to select their character from a simple character-select screen shown before the game starts. Each playable character must have a unique name, distinct 3D mesh with unique materials/colors in Character3D.tsx, and slightly different base stats (e.g., speed, health, attack power) reflected in the Character type in frontend/src/types/game.ts.

Extend the Character interface and level configuration types in frontend/src/types/game.ts to support multiple character types. Add a 'characterType' string field (or enum) to the Character interface to differentiate between character variants (e.g., 'ug', 'newPlayer', 'sukuna', 'newEnemy1', 'newEnemy2'). Update LEVEL_CONFIGS or equivalent constants to specify which enemy types appear in each level.
